mnemonics: Regular matrices are POSITIVE: P (for Positive) O (for One) S (for Stochastic) I (for Irreducible) T (for Transition) I (for Inevitable) V (for Vector) E (for Exists).
keyPoints: A stochastic matrix is regular if some power of the matrix has all positive entries (no zeros).
Regular Markov chains are guaranteed to converge to a unique stationary distribution, regardless of the starting state.
The Perron-Frobenius theorem guarantees that for a regular stochastic matrix, 1 is the largest eigenvalue and the corresponding eigenvector (the stationary distribution) has all positive entries.
commonMistakes: Confusing 'regular' with 'irreducible' or 'ergodic'. A matrix can be irreducible but not regular if it is periodic.
Assuming that because a matrix is stochastic, it must be regular. Many stochastic matrices (like those with absorbing states) are not regular.
Forgetting to check *all* entries are positive in some power P^k. A single zero entry in every power means the matrix is not regular.
